monitors.name: Council of Europe AI Convention Treaty Office status
monitors.sourceLabel: Council of Europe Treaty Office, CETS No. 225
monitors.currentAppClaim: CETS No. 225 is represented as signed by the listed states and organizations, ratified by the EU on 15 May 2026, and not yet in force because five ratifications including at least three Council of Europe member states are required.

expectedValues.label: ratifications/accessions
expectedValues.recommendedAction: Review ratified participation rows and the convention in-force caveat.

expectedValues.label: signatures not followed by ratifications
expectedValues.pattern: Total number of signatures not followed by ratifications\s*\|\s*(\d+)
expectedValues.recommendedAction: Review signed participation rows for CETS No. 225.

manualReviewPatterns.label: JavaScript-only Treaty Office shell
manualReviewPatterns.pattern: You need to enable JavaScript to run this app
manualReviewPatterns.recommendedAction: Open the Treaty Office page manually or with a browser-rendered checker and compare signatures, ratifications, and entry-into-force status.

manualVerification.observedStatus: Manual review of official Council of Europe Treaty Office and AI news pages confirms the current app claim: Treaty 225 has 19 signatures not followed by ratification, 1 ratification/accession, the EU ratified on 15 May 2026, and the treaty is not yet in force.
evidence: Council of Europe Treaty Office CETS No. 225 status excerpt reports status as of 22/05/2026, 19 signatures not followed by ratifications, 1 ratification/accession, and entry into force requiring 5 ratifications including at least 3 Council of Europe member states.
Council of Europe AI news page states that the European Union ratified CETS No. 225 on 15 May 2026.
Council of Europe AI news page states that North Macedonia signed CETS No. 225 on 8 May 2026, bringing the total number of signatories up to 20.
The app's 20 signed participation rows include the European Union; the separate EU ratified row records the 15 May 2026 ratification.
manualVerification.recommendedAction: No repo action needed until 2026-07-05 unless the Treaty Office page shows a newer signature, ratification, or entry-into-force status.

monitors.name: EU AI Act EUR-Lex status
monitors.currentAppClaim: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 is the EU AI Act, entered into force on 1 August 2024, with phased application across EU member states.

changeSignals.label: possible repeal/end-of-validity language
changeSignals.pattern: repealed|no longer in force|end of validity
changeSignals.recommendedAction: Review EU AI Act status and update binding-law summaries only if EUR-Lex confirms a legal-status change.

manualVerification.observedStatus: Manual review of the official EUR-Lex Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 page confirms the current app claim: the document is Regulation (EU) 2024/1689, the Artificial Intelligence Act, published in OJ L 2024/1689 on 12 July 2024, and marked in force.
evidence: EUR-Lex page identifies Document 32024R1689 and Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 of 13 June 2024 laying down harmonised rules on artificial intelligence.
EUR-Lex page title includes Artificial Intelligence Act.
EUR-Lex page shows OJ L, 2024/1689, 12.7.2024, with ELI http://data.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/1689/oj.
EUR-Lex page legal-status display says In force.
manualVerification.recommendedAction: No repo action needed until 2026-07-13 unless EUR-Lex shows a newer consolidation, corrigendum, or legal-status change.

monitors.name: OECD AI Principles legal-instrument status
monitors.currentAppClaim: OECD/LEGAL/0449 is the OECD Recommendation on AI / OECD AI Principles, originally adopted in 2019 and revised for generative AI in 2024.

requiredPatterns.label: OECD legal identifier
requiredPatterns.pattern: OECD/LEGAL/0449|Recommendation of the Council on Artificial Intelligence|Recommendation on Artificial Intelligence

expectedValues.label: last amendment date
expectedValues.recommendedAction: Review the OECD AI Principles summary and timeline if the legal instrument has been amended again.

manualReviewPatterns.label: JavaScript-only OECD shell
manualReviewPatterns.pattern: You need to enable JavaScript to run this app
manualReviewPatterns.recommendedAction: Open the OECD Legal Instruments page manually and compare the title, legal identifier, adherent status, and revision history.

monitors.name: GPAI declarations and ministerial statements
monitors.currentAppClaim: GPAI declarations and ministerial statements are voluntary policy-coordination forum outputs, not binding instruments.

monitors.name: UNESCO Recommendation on AI ethics
monitors.currentAppClaim: UNESCO adopted the Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence on 23 November 2021; it is a non-binding standard-setting instrument.

requiredPatterns.label: AI ethics recommendation
requiredPatterns.pattern: ethics of artificial intelligence|Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence

monitors.name: UNGA Resolution 78/265 official document record
monitors.currentAppClaim: A/RES/78/265 is the UN General Assembly resolution on safe, secure and trustworthy AI systems for sustainable development, adopted on 21 March 2024.

monitors.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
monitors.currentAppClaim: NIST AI RMF 1.0 is voluntary U.S. technical guidance with international influence, not binding law.

requiredPatterns.label: NIST identity
requiredPatterns.pattern: NIST|National Institute of Standards and Technology

monitors.name: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile
monitors.currentAppClaim: The NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile is voluntary U.S. technical guidance, not an international agreement or binding national rule.

requiredPatterns.label: NIST identity
requiredPatterns.pattern: NIST|National Institute of Standards and Technology

requiredPatterns.label: GenAI Profile identity
requiredPatterns.pattern: Generative Artificial Intelligence Profile|Generative AI Profile|AI RMF
